rows.old_rule_text: Inspect draft, outline, notes, source material, prior posts, templates, house style, venue instructions, and supplied examples before drafting.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Inspect available writing context before drafting or revising.

rows.old_rule_text: Preserve the claim ledger; keep caveats visible; flag unsupported claims.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Separate claims, evidence, assumptions, caveats, and gaps.

rows.old_rule_text: Use short, precise sentences. Replace vague phrases, unexplained metaphors, and overloaded local names.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Use short precise sentences and common terminology.

rows.old_rule_text: Define every concept before its first use.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Define non-common concepts before first use.

rows.old_rule_text: Prefer inline equations unless display equations are important.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Prefer inline equations unless display is needed.

rows.old_rule_text: Finalizable writing workflows must record style_profile_ref and style_applied.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Record active style profile for finalizable writing.

rows.old_rule_text: Capture new durable user writing requirements and do not promote them without approval.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Capture new durable user style requirements without silent promotion.

rows.old_rule_text: Put concepts and notation used several times in preliminaries; define one-use concepts locally.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Define mathematical concepts and notation before first use.

rows.old_rule_text: Never mix statements and definitions. Do not define anything inside a statement.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Do not define notation inside mathematical statements.

rows.old_rule_text: Prefer standard graph-theoretic and TCS terminology over private vocabulary.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Use common graph-theoretic and TCS terminology.

rows.old_rule_text: Restate hypotheses in major results when doing so makes the result self-contained.
Add one to three sentences before the statement explaining what the result says and why it is needed.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Introduce important statements with their role.

rows.old_rule_text: Each section except Introduction and Concluding remarks should contain a short outline paragraph.
Start every long or technical proof with a short paragraph giving the proof idea.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Open long proofs with proof ideas.

rows.old_rule_text: For equations, except those which are important, prefer inline equations.
rows.normalized_rule_text: Prefer inline equations unless display is needed.
